rationale:
Reviewed against frozen v15 Task 4a scope (v12 + AM-40 + AM-41). Findings per objective clause:

(a) Closed inventory — SATISFIED. PACKAGE_ROOTS is exactly the five spine packages, PACKAGE_ROOTS_EXCLUDE exactly the three sibling products, both sorted/unique, with the AM-40 mechanical completeness unit (test-inventory §7) proving discovered == union, disjointness, and exact arrays over git-tracked package.json (correctly using tracked files, not a filesystem scan; node_modules filtered; a tracked repo-root package.json asserted absent rather than silently dropped). DOC_ROOTS, docs/runs and self-weave exclusions committed with reviewed exact values. CONTRACT_FILES sorted and existence-checked (D31). WEAVERS carries the five ids with integer versions. REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS deep-equals the frozen normative table with a unit asserting equality, sorted/unique per weaver, deep-frozen. LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS deep-equals the D32 normative mapping with exact-key/exact-value, deep-freeze (outer + both inner arrays), and mutation-fails-and-unchanged units. LEDGER_SOURCES=[] is committed as an exact reviewed value with a rationale, not a placeholder — a legitimate closed-inventory decision at this SHA.

(b) D17/AM-17 — SATISFIED. WEAVER_IMPL_FILES commits only git and code closures; every named file exists (test-inventory §4 stats each impl file and permitted external); no inventory names a nonexistent file.

(c) util.mjs substrate — SATISFIED. ONE classifier (classifyModuleLoads) + ONE resolver (resolveRelativeSpecifier) exported and consumed by both deriveAcceptedClosure and code.mjs, with test-closure §9 mechanically proving single-definition-in-util, code.mjs importing the shared resolver, and deriveAcceptedClosure's body calling both (AM-34 test 19 satisfied for the consumers that exist at this PR; the Task 5 scanner is future by plan). Lexer is dependency-free, comment/string/template/regex-aware, never executes code; Phase-1 export grammar is exactly the four forms with warnings for every unsupported category (each export keyword dispatched). Walker rejects root escape and symlinked input (throws on symlinked entries rather than skipping — fail-closed, consistent with 'rejects rather than following'). Counted iterator counts only on completed consumption, exhaustion only on natural completion, accounting only via the driver-held accessor (units prove non-reachability from the iterable). physicalContainment lstats every component including ancestors and the AM-41(c) original-uncollapsed order, never follows symlinks to decide. Git wrapper permits exactly the five frozen shapes via execFileSync with shell:false; caller options dropped; GIT_* redirect env neutralized (proven against real git in test-util §18b); path args restricted to canonical repo-relative non-option-shaped tokens.

AM-41 profile — SATISFIED. One optional leading shebang admitted (offset 0, LF/CRLF, stripped); b1–b6 each mechanically detected and rejected with the stable unsupported-module-lexical-profile diagnostic, each covered by units (bare CR/U+2028/U+2029; misplaced/second hashbang; <!-- and line-leading --> with the full horizontal-ws set; line-continuation and legacy/octal escapes; string-literal specifier/alias names incl. export * as "ns"; unterminated strings/comments/templates/regex and truncated accepted forms incl. unclosed calls and unbalanced import options). Supported-profile correctness under whitespace/comment/alias/options/token-context variation is extensively unit-pinned, including regex-vs-division by previous significant token (control-head parens, block-vs-object braces, value keywords vs reserved words, postfix ++/--, of-in-control-head).

(d) test-closure.mjs — SATISFIED. Committed inventories proven equal to derived closures with the shared classifier/resolver through the same harness as the failure fixtures; per-form omission fixtures for all seven accepted forms (+ keyword-alias and whitespace module.require variants); recursive mixed chain; cycle exactly-once; unreachable literal dynamic import included; permitted merkle-dag helper recursively traversed; missing/extra/nonexistent-inventory failures; unresolved/ambiguous/non-regular/escape/forbidden/symlink fatal targets; entry subject to same checks; non-literal and comment/string lookalikes no-edge; direct real-repo closure equality (§12) additionally proves the exact fixed points and that registry/inventory are leaves; require-style non-.mjs targets traverse per D33 (§13); out-of-profile members fail the whole closure (§14); allowExternal cannot widen beyond merkle-dag/ (§15).

(e) git.mjs — SATISFIED. Exactly the two frozen log invocations, first-result (earliest, --reverse) selection, source_ref git:<same-sha>, every line validated as full SHA (CRLF tolerated as terminators, bare CR fatal, blank lines fatal, empty output warns no-edge), structured producer-attributed warnings, own-input validation before argv splice proven with a spy runner, closure intent {registry,git} pinned. The -S<symbol> verbatim-token unit covers metacharacter safety.

(f) code.mjs — SATISFIED. All four depends-on endpoint shapes with symbol- vs file-level threading; no-export used named import preserved as repository-file -> code-symbol; namespace/side-effect/default and named-not-seeded thread at file level; unrepresentable-consumer ONLY for unresolvable specifiers (missing .mjs or resolves outside closed roots), with the non-.mjs-missing-file precedence pinned as silent; unused imports and comment/string/regex occurrences emit no edge; dedup; locators carry repository_ref + blob_sha both levels; byte-equal {edges,warnings} over two runs; symlink/escape targets fail closed rather than downgrade.

Zero runtime deps, ESM, node:-stdlib + clotho-relative + permitted vendor imports only, diff confined to clotho/ — all verified by the deterministic gate. Spine read-only respected.

Judgment notes (why these are not defects): the code-weaver throwing on symlink/escape import targets is a fail-closed physical-policy posture consistent with D21/D33 and pinned by tests, and the task text's unrepresentable/unused clauses are still exactly honored; walker throwing on symlinked entries is the strict reading of 'rejects rather than following'; LEDGER_SOURCES=[] and RUN_SOURCES single-entry are reviewed exact values whose paths are existence-checked. Residual risks are bounded and non-blocking: the hand-rolled lexer's correctness rests on the AM-41 closed profile plus its extensive adversarial unit battery (the design explicitly trades a complete ES lexer for total correctness over an enforced subset, per the ruling), and full-suite green is attested by the gate (test-all, 7 tests) rather than re-executed by this seat — hence confidence medium rather than high.
